objective: Turn the bitmesh composition-certificate contract into an auditable hard-target shard with strict component provenance, composition-aware OOD splits, and baselines that can test whether decomposition helps beyond full-board hand features.
non_negotiables: Exact composed rows require a validated strict decomposition digest, one verified exact value digest per component root, and a deterministic composed result digest.
A composition certificate is a provenance binding, not a value proof by itself; generators must still verify component values and composed result values.
Weak, unchecked, stale, duplicate-root, or missing-digest decompositions remain rejected controls and must not enter exact supervision.

Every composition OOD report must include raw-position, D4-symmetry, decomposition-certificate, composition-certificate, generator-family, component-count, component-family, and target-support leakage checks.
Any decomposition-benefit claim must compare against full-board scalar, no-decomposition structured, fixed FEN/material, and hand-geometry baselines on the same split.
Temperature, agency, or beauty claims remain inactive unless exact nonconstant targets beat train-majority and hand-feature controls under the composition split.

tasks.prompt: Freeze the BMCOMPOSE v1 composition-certificate payload added in bitmesh commit c6e8e4d. Add any missing negative controls for duplicate roots, empty digests, stale decomposition digests, and component-count canonicalization. Document exactly what the certificate proves and what it does not prove.
outputs: Stable composition-certificate payload contract
Negative-control tests for malformed composition provenance
Contract notes for astralbase and partizan integration
acceptance: Validated certificates digest deterministically before any downstream row can cite them
Malformed composition metadata fails before digesting
Documentation says the composition certificate binds provenance but does not independently verify chess values

tasks.prompt: Expose or document the canonical exact-value digest contract that astralbase should use for component values and composed results. Preserve existing number and switch byte stability, and make unsupported non-number chess values explicit rather than approximate.
outputs: Exact-value digest adapter or documented stable API
Golden tests for number, switch, and game-tree payloads used as composition components
Compatibility notes for BMCOMPOSE value_digest fields
acceptance: Canonical value digests are stable across runs
Existing exact payloads remain compatible
Unsupported targets fail explicitly instead of producing heuristic labels

tasks.prompt: Build the deterministic composition hard-target generator. Emit exact rows only when component decomposition, component exact values, and composed result values are all verified. Emit rejected controls for weak decomposition, missing component proofs, unsupported values, duplicate roots, and stale composition digests.
outputs: Composition hard-target generator CLI
Exact rows with decomposition and composition certificate digests
Rejected rows with explicit unsupported reason codes
Tests for deterministic generation, family coverage, and malformed-certificate rejection
acceptance: Two generator runs produce byte-identical JSONL
Exact rows carry component value digests and a composed result digest
Rejected controls cannot be loaded as exact targets

tasks.prompt: Wire the composition shard into partizan. Extend schema fixtures and orchestration so exact rows can carry nested decomposition and composition certificate provenance while rejected controls remain excluded from exact supervision.
outputs: Wave 17 JSONL artifact and manifest
Schema fixtures for valid and invalid composition certificate rows
Orchestrator command for deterministic composition shard generation
Smoke tests for exact/rejected separation
commands: python3 -m py_compile engine/ml_model.py engine/test.py engine/orchestrator.py agents/network.py agents/label_schema.py
acceptance: Schema-valid exact composition rows require complete provenance
Missing or malformed composition certificates fail validation or are rejected controls
The manifest cites exact bitmesh, thermograph, astralbase, and partizan commits

tasks.prompt: Add composition-aware split and target-support reports. Required split modes: D4 symmetry, component-count holdout, component-family holdout, and composition-certificate holdout. Reports must fail loudly on cross-split raw-position, symmetry, decomposition-certificate, or composition-certificate leakage.
outputs: Composition split report
Component-family and component-count holdout reports
Exact-only target-support coverage for composed result fields
Updated experiment matrix entries
acceptance: Every split records composition certificate counts and leakage summaries
Unseen target labels are reported rather than hidden
Reports exclude rejected controls from exact-value targets

tasks.prompt: Implement the first decomposition-aware baseline and its controls. Compare against full-board scalar, no-decomposition structured, fixed FEN/material, train-majority, and hand-geometry baselines on the exact same composition OOD split.
outputs: Decomposition-aware baseline report
No-decomposition and scalar-control ablations
Failure analysis when hand features or train-majority match the structured model
acceptance: Metrics include train/dev/test plus held-out composition slices
No decomposition claim activates unless the decomposition-aware model beats controls
The report records exact target support and rejected-row exclusion

tasks.prompt: Audit Wave 17 for overclaiming. Invalidate any agency, beauty, decomposition, or temperature claim not supported by exact labels, composition certificates, leakage-free OOD reports, and baseline-beating evidence.
outputs: Claim ledger update
Reviewer-risk notes for composition certificates
Concrete blockers for the next wave
acceptance: Every activated claim has evidence, baseline, and falsification condition
Composition certificates are not overstated as full chess independence proofs
The next wave has measurable blockers rather than vague research intent

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_17_composition_certificates.json
integration_gates.success_signal: The network and Wave 17 composition plan are internally consistent.

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/label_schema.py self-test && python3 engine/test.py && python3 -m py_compile engine/ml_model.py engine/test.py engine/orchestrator.py agents/network.py agents/label_schema.py
integration_gates.success_signal: Partizan schema, reporting, and orchestration smoke gates pass.

integration_gates.command: cd ../bitmesh && cargo test && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cd ../thermograph && cargo test && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cd ../astralbase && cargo test && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
integration_gates.success_signal: All engine crates pass tests and clippy after composition integration.
